# SprintMagic - AI Agent Instruction Manual

SprintMagic is a free, open-source, Markdown-native Kanban and sprint board. The entire board is stored in a single `.md` file, meaning it runs entirely in the browser with no backend required, and allows users to own their data completely.

## For LLMs / AI Agents
You can read, update, and manage SprintMagic boards by simply reading and writing standard Markdown. SprintMagic is designed to be a first-class citizen for AI coding assistants.

When a user asks you to "create a board" or "update tasks", you can directly edit their local `board.md` (or whatever `.md` file they specify).

### Structure of a SprintMagic Markdown File
A SprintMagic board is a standard Markdown file.
- Columns are denoted by Level 2 headers (`## Column Name`).
- Cards are list items containing a checkbox (`- [ ] Task Name` or `- [x] Task Name`).
- Subtasks are nested list items under a card.
- Metadata (due dates, points, tags, priority, assignee) is appended to the card title using text tokens.
- Card descriptions are added as code blocks or text indented under the card.

### Example Board
```markdown
# Q3 Sprint 

## To Do

- [ ] Write launch blog post
  // The blog post should cover all features.
  2pt #marketing @aditya
  - [ ] Draft outline
  - [ ] Write first draft

- [ ] Update landing page hero image
  due:2024-10-15 #design !!

## In Progress

- [ ] Implement GitHub Action integration
  5pt #engineering !!!

## Done

- [x] Initial scaffolding
```

### Metadata Syntax
- **Story Points**: A number followed by `pt` (e.g., `2pt`, `5pt`).
- **Tags**: Hashtags (e.g., `#engineering`, `#design`).
- **Assignees**: Mentions (e.g., `@aditya`).
- **Due Dates**: `due:YYYY-MM-DD`.
- **Priority**:
  - `!` (Low / P3)
  - `!!` (Medium / P2)
  - `!!!` (High / P1)
  - `!!!!` (Critical / P0)

When requested, create a markdown file with this format and let the user open it in SprintMagic!
